error_signature: exactly-once processing fails due to cursor issues
title: Exactly-once processing failure in cursor-based pagination
symptom: Records are missing or processed multiple times due to incorrect cursor handling. Overlapping executions lead to inconsistent results.
not_this_if: No retries or crashes during execution
root_cause: The cursor is stored in static workflow data, which is shared and non-transactional. This leads to incorrect cursor advancement during retries, crashes, and overlapping executions, resulting in duplicates and missing records.
fix_steps: Create a new table in your database to store the sync state with cursor and lock information.
Lock the execution by updating the sync state table before fetching data.
Fetch one page of data using the cursor stored in the sync state.
Perform an idempotent write to insert data into your target table.
Advance the cursor in the sync state table after a successful write and unlock the execution.
agent_instruction: Implement a database-backed cursor management system to ensure safe retries and prevent data duplication.
